title: rhododendron punctifolium
intro: Rhododendron punctifolium, also known as the pondberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of pondberry include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Rhododendron punctifolium can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
